title: Mosai (Forcura) & Mosai Circle — Intake & Order Management
summary: Deployed Mosai (formerly Forcura) for clinical intake and order management across field and office staff, plus Mosai Circle for inter-organizational physician and facility eSignatures. Eliminated paper-based intake and accelerated order turnaround for every referring physician partner.
problem: Intake and physician order management ran on paper, fax, and follow-up phone calls. Order turnaround was bounded by the speed of fax and the availability of the prescribing physician at a desk to sign. The clinical workflow had no audit trail and no path to integration with the EHR.
approach: Mapped the existing intake and order workflow end to end — referral source, intake review, physician order, signature, EHR documentation
Configured Mosai for field and office intake roles with workflows aligned to the actual clinical paths — not a vendor's template
Stood up Mosai Circle for inter-organizational eSignatures with referring physician practices and partner facilities
Assessed the deployment against HIPAA data handling requirements and 42 CFR Part 418 hospice clinical record requirements before go-live
Trained intake coordinators, field clinicians, and physician office staff on the new digital flow
lessons: Healthcare workflow tools succeed in proportion to how closely they match the clinical workflow as it actually runs — not the workflow described in the policy manual. Spend time shadowing intake coordinators and field clinicians before you configure anything. Also: inter-organizational eSignature only works when the partner physician practices are on board, which means relationship work as much as technology work — start that conversation weeks before deployment.
